headlines.title: Burnham's chancellor will have to find extra £4.7bn for defence, says minister
headlines.description: Luke Pollard says the next chancellor "whoever that may be" will have to "find the resources" to fund defence plan.

headlines.title: Woman raped by Superdry co-founder tells BBC she was working for him at the time
headlines.description: She described feeling fear and dread as she returned to her job, working for James Holder, after the attack.

headlines.title: Nowak case officers face gross misconduct investigation
headlines.description: Henry Nowak, 18, was handcuffed as he lay dying after being stabbed by Vickrum Digwa in December 2025.

headlines.title: Why Gen Z are planning for life without a state pension
headlines.description: Many younger people do not believe the state pension will exist when they are older

headlines.title: Family of boy attacked by crocodile thank zoo staff who rescued him
headlines.description: The boy remains at Addenbrooke's Hospital in a stable condition, police say.

headlines.title: Households urged to submit meter readings as energy prices rise
headlines.description: Household energy prices have risen by 13% a year as regulator Ofgem's latest price cap kicks in.

headlines.title: Trump made more than $1bn from crypto in first year back in office
headlines.description: The president's crypto income far outpaces his earnings from real estate and Trump-themed items such as watches.

headlines.title: Women with PMOS should have yearly NHS checks, says health watchdog
headlines.description: Despite affecting millions of women in the UK, PMOS is still under-diagnosed and inconsistently managed, say experts.

headlines.title: Brewery boss who banned phones and swearing from pubs dies aged 81
headlines.description: Humphrey Smith had been at the helm of the Tadcaster brewer since the 1980s.

headlines.title: Chris Brown ordered to pay housekeeper $13m after dog attack
headlines.description: Maria Aliva was left with scarring, vision loss and nerve damage following the attack in December 2020.

headlines.title: Time for Tuchel to show hand as England enter World Cup danger zone
headlines.description: England coach Thomas Tuchel must now play his strongest hand as he enters the danger zone in a World Cup of shocks, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

headlines.title: Knockouts are here - what is England's path to the final?
headlines.description: BBC Sport takes a look at what England will need to do to reach the final after qualifying for the knockout stages.

headlines.title: Mexico rampant at fortress Azteca - and England could be next
headlines.description: Mexico have won all four games and not conceded a goal at this World Cup. Their last-16 opponents must also contend with playing at the Azteca Stadium.

headlines.title: United, free and brilliant - can anyone stop France masterclass?
headlines.description: France boss Didier Deschamps urges caution despite watching his side dismantle Sweden to leave Les Bleus fans dreaming of winning the World Cup.

headlines.title: A plane crashed into a tower in Beijing but China is not saying what happened
headlines.description: The collision left holes on the side of the tower, but Beijing has scrubbed other visible traces of the incident.

headlines.title: It looks like an ordinary house - but our tropical garden is packed with poisonous plants
headlines.description: The garden was once a sun trap with decking - it is now filled with tropical trees and ferns shading it.

headlines.title: Chris Mason: Starmer's defence plan leaves crunching trade‑offs for Burnham to confront
headlines.description: The BBC's political editor Chris Mason reflects on the fallout from the Defence Investment Plan.

headlines.title: Is it really happening this weekend? What we do and don't know about Taylor Swift's wedding
headlines.description: The couple has been mum about their upcoming nuptials, but speculation has mounted about a multi-day celebration at Madison Square Garden.

headlines.title: A trip to India left me with 38 parasites in my brain
headlines.description: Lowri's headaches, seizures and psychosis were due to an infection caused by pork tapeworm larvae.

headlines.title: 'We're still standing': Americans on the state of the nation in its 250th year
headlines.description: The BBC spoke to US citizens across the country ahead of its big anniversary celebration this weekend.

headlines.title: Can China target critics abroad with its new 'ethnic unity' law?
headlines.description: Rights groups fear the new law gives Beijing legal authority to go after people beyond its borders.

headlines.title: Village People frontman Victor Willis dies aged 74
headlines.description: Victor Willis was the band's frontman and co-wrote most of their hit songs including YMCA.

headlines.title: E-scooters being advertised for commuting despite UK road ban
headlines.description: A Press Association investigation found major retailers advertising e-scooters for "commuting" and "urban riding".

headlines.title: Three killed in Mexico City World Cup celebrations
headlines.description: More than one million people took to the streets of Mexico City on Tuesday to mark the country's win over Ecuador.

headlines.title: Patient tests negative after Ebola alert at Glasgow hospital
headlines.description: Precautionary measures were taken after the patient was admitted to the Queen Elizabeth University Hospital early on Tuesday.

headlines.title: 'I spend 19 hours in bed': Home care pressures leave 89-year-old 'missing freedom'
headlines.description: Alison said carers now give her an evening meal and medication during an afternoon call at about 15:00 BST and help her to bed for the night.

headlines.title: Girl reveals rape trauma ahead of sentence review
headlines.description: The teenager is one of two girls who were raped by two boys in Fordingbridge, Hampshire.

headlines.title: Three-year-old rescued and taken to hospital six days after Venezuela quake
headlines.description: Video footage shows rescuers cheering as the child is pulled from rubble in La Guaira state.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: What Does It Cost To Keep The UK Safe?
headlines.description: The government releases it’s long awaited defence investment plan.

headlines.title: Has the Supreme Court just made Trump the most powerful president in history?
headlines.description: A landmark decision expands Trump’s presidential power

headlines.description: Mexico have won all four games and not conceded a goal at this World Cup. Their last-16 opponents must also contend with playing at the Azteca Stadium.

headlines.title: United, free and brilliant - can anyone stop France masterclass?
headlines.description: France boss Didier Deschamps urges caution despite watching his side dismantle Sweden to leave Les Bleus fans dreaming of winning the World Cup.

headlines.title: 120mph serves but movement a struggle - how did Williams' return go?
headlines.description: Serena Williams loses on her singles return to Wimbledon - but what can we expect next from the tennis legend?

headlines.title: Koeman resigns, while Netherlands report racist abuse
headlines.description: Ronald Koeman resigns as Netherlands manager after their World Cup exit, which led to "appalling" racist abuse aimed at players who missed penalties.
